svg.title: Nginx security — TLS termination and centralized port protection
svg.desc: Clients connect to nginx over HTTPS on port 443. Nginx terminates TLS and forwards plain HTTP to backend servers on internal ports. A firewall boundary shows that only port 443 is exposed publicly while backend ports are hidden.

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How does HTTPS protect the client connection?')
sendPrompt('How does nginx handle TLS termination?')
sendPrompt('Why don't backend servers need their own TLS certificates?')

svg.text: One TLS certificate to manage, not one per server
Backend servers never handle encryption overhead
Internal ports hidden from the public internet
